description: Figure 5 presents four transmission electron microscopy (TEM) dark-field (DF) images with corresponding selected-area diffraction patterns (SADPs) illustrating the microstructural evolution of ferrite in Fe–28Mn–9Al-1.2C-xCr austenitic low-density steel strips with varying chromium content (x = 0, 3, 6, 9 wt%). Subfigures (a) and (b) show DF images of 0Cr and 3Cr strips using (110)δ and (1$\overline{1}$0)δ reflections, respectively, with SADPs along the [111]δ zone axis exhibiting only fundamental BCC reflections, indicating clean δ-ferrite without secondary phases. In contrast, subfigures (c) and (d) display DF images of 6Cr and 9Cr strips using (200)DO3 superlattice reflections, with SADPs showing both δ-ferrite and DO3 ordered phase patterns (zone axes [001] and [011], respectively). The presence of superlattice spots such as (100) and (200) in (c) and (d) confirms the formation of nano-scale DO3 precipitates within the ferrite matrix in high-Cr alloys, whereas these ordered structures are absent in the 0Cr and 3Cr samples. The images reveal a critical Cr-content threshold for DO3 phase formation in ferrite, supporting the correlation between Cr addition and ordered phase precipitation.
